findings.title: Hardcoded JWT secret exposes authentication to attack
findings.description: The JWT signing secret is a hardcoded string constant. Anyone who reads the source code can forge valid JWTs and impersonate any user. Use environment variables.

findings.title: Broken admin role check — compares username instead of role
findings.description: The adminOnly guard checks if user.username === 'admin' which is a broken security check. It should verify a roles array or an isAdmin flag.

findings.title: SQL injection via string interpolation in getUserData
findings.description: The userId parameter is directly interpolated into a SQL string without sanitization or parameterization. This is a textbook SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.suggestedFix: return db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);

findings.title: SQL injection in DELETE endpoint
findings.description: The req.params.id value is interpolated into a SQL DELETE statement without sanitization.
findings.suggestedFix: await db.query('DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1', [id]);

findings.title: IDOR vulnerability — missing ownership check on delete
findings.description: Any authenticated user can delete any other user's account by supplying a different user ID in the URL. There is no check that the requesting user owns the account being deleted.
